v3.15.6 NRFI math fix: True NRFI = P(away SP holds home team) × P(home SP holds away team), now multiplied (joint probability) instead of averaged. Market avg ~55%, model avg now matches range. YRFI bandbox/leaky triggers per §14.3 surfaced separately.

🎯 1st-Inning ML — 1 games triggered §14.1 (highest-EV NRFI play)

Bet the team facing the weaker SP. WIN if your team scores 1st, PUSH if NRFI, LOSS only if opponent scores while yours doesn't.
GamePickPitcher RolesΔOutcomeTier
PIT @ PHI
Citizens Bank Park		Bet PHI
facing Paul Skenes
Backing: Zack Wheeler (100% role, 5 starts)
Attacked: Paul Skenes (57% role, 7 starts)
Δ 43ppW 35% · L 0% · P 65%B

⚠️ YRFI Picks — 2 games (bet AT LEAST 1 RUN in 1st inning)

YRFI bandbox = Park Runs ≥+10% with market YRFI implied ≥55%. YRFI leaky = both SPs ≤50% NRFI in role + park R ≥+5%. Bet: at least one run in 1st.

§2.2 K Over rubric: c1=2026 K-Over ≥60%, c3=L10 ≥60%, c4=K% ≥22% OR p/K ≤16. F11 spray filter active. §10 projection uses per-batter K%s × 22 BF × opp_K_mult.
📖 How to read: Proj K% / Mod K% = our model's chance the pitcher goes over his strikeout line today (now built from the actual 9 hitters he faces). True % = the graded probability; Edge = where the market looks mispriced; Grade = rubric strength. Most strikeouts likely → sort by Mod K% desc. Best value → sort Edge desc within A/B+.

§2.1 HR rubric: c1=2026 HR% ≥30%, c2=L10 HR% ≥50% (v3.8 floor), c3=Park HR ≥0%, c4=P wOBA vs-hand ≥.340 OR HR/9 vs-hand ≥1.5, c5=platoon (PA≥30 + brl≥10% + xwOBACON≥.400). Filters F1/F6/F7/F9/F10 active. BvP poison (0-for-15+ → ×0.70). §10 proj: (l10Avg×0.55 + 2026rate×0.45) × park × F7 × F9 × spray × BvP.
📖 How to read: True % = how likely this prop actually hits today (model's blended probability). Edge = where the market is mispriced vs True %. Grade = how many of the 4–5 rubric criteria the player passed. For "who's most likely to homer" → sort by True % desc. For "best betting value" → sort by Edge desc (within A/B+ grades only).

🏃 Stolen Base Candidates — a ranking to research, not graded bets. There's no rubric grade or edge here; it's our best guess at who's in a good spot to steal today, built from speed, recent steals, the runner's jump, how often his team runs, and how easy the opposing pitcher/catcher are to run on. The weights are estimates we haven't yet tuned to real results.
📖 How to read this tab — in plain English:
How good a base-stealer is he?  Sprint = top speed (29+ ft/s is fast) · Bolts = how many all-out sprints (context only) · SB Over% = props.cash's season OVER-RATE (% of games he cleared the 0.5 SB line) — a rate, NOT a steal count (this source has no raw SB total) · SB Vol = sample-gated per-game steal volume (steals/game shrunk toward league baseline so a tiny-sample high rate can't outrank a proven full-season stealer) — this is the volume the Scores use · l10 / l20% = how often he's stolen in recent games · Sec Lead / Lead Δ = how big a lead/jump he takes off the base (bigger = more aggressive).
How easy is it to run in this game?  Team SB = how much his whole team runs (green light) · P-Hold & P-SB = how easy the opposing starter is to steal on (P-SB higher = easier, the main signal; P-Hold is a smaller current-year tweak) · Sea SB/9 & Sea SB-CS = the opposing starter's CURRENT-SEASON steals-allowed per 9 IP and his raw SB-CS this year (MLB.com) — the this-season read alongside P-SB's multi-year rate; "—" = that starter isn't on the season board (no steals allowed / not scraped) · Att% = props.cash's attempt-rate allowed — a PITCHER number: how often runners even try to steal vs today's starter (lower = the pitcher shuts the run game down; e.g. 0.3% = almost nobody runs, 1.7%+ = runnable; "—" just means props.cash didn't publish one for this matchup) · PC SB = props.cash's own over/under lean + 0–100 matchup grade on THIS runner stealing (Over / higher grade = better steal spot) — a separate, runner-level verdict that can be present even when Att% shows "—", which is why a "—" Att% row can still look like a likely steal. Both Att% and PC SB are props.cash's own metrics, context only — neither is in the Score. · PK = the opposing starter's season pickoffs (MLB.com) — a different way he controls the run game; context only. · e2B / e3B = evanalytics' modeled chance a runner both tries and succeeds stealing 2nd / 3rd vs this starter (higher = more runnable; the 2B-vs-3B split is the useful part) — a third-party model, context only, not in the Score. · A * on a P-SB number means it's a current-season backfill from MLB.com (used only when the multi-year Razzball board had no sample for that pitcher), not the multi-year read. · Reach = how likely he reaches base to even attempt a steal · wOBA / xwOBA = overall hitter quality (context only).
The bottom line:  Score = our overall "good spot to steal" number (higher = better). Over = today's steal-prop odds. Click ★ to save a runner to 💎 My Plays; use the Team/Game filters to focus one matchup.
🆕 Alt Score + the Recent-weight slider: Alt Score ranks the same runners but leans on durable raw skill (speed, jump, steal efficiency) instead of recent hot/cold streaks. Use the Recent wt (Alt) slider in the filter bar to decide how much recent form counts: drag it to 0 to see pure raw-metric placement, or up to let recent form dominate — it re-ranks live. The plain Score stays put so you can compare. Both are un-fitted estimates; we log them so real outcomes can eventually tell us which ranks better.
🆕 Opposing-pitcher CURRENT-SEASON SB is in the table below — two columns, Sea SB/9 and Sea SB-CS, sit immediately to the RIGHT of the P-SB column (this table is wide — scroll it sideways if you don't see them). A standalone "SB Allowed — current season" table is also at the very bottom of this tab. · board rendered 2026-07-01 16:36. If you do NOT see this blue line, you are viewing an older saved copy of the board — reopen the newest file.

🏃 SB Allowed — current season (MLB.com)

The newly-scraped current-season stolen-base-allowed numbers for every starter today — including low-IP / just-up arms the multi-year Razzball read has no row for. SB/CS = steals / caught-stealings allowed this year · PK = pickoffs (a different way he kills the run game) · CS% = how often he throws runners out · SB/9 = steals allowed per 9 IP (this feeds the runner board's P-SB when Razzball has no sample). Read bands are heuristic SB/9 (league median ≈ 0.61): RUN ≥ 1.20 · lean ≥ 0.90 · neutral ≥ 0.50 · tough ≥ 0.25 · AVOID below. Click a pitcher → runners most likely to steal vs him.

📖 Cheat Sheets — today's starting pitchers, ranked. Reference only — no grades, no bets. Each sheet ranks today's starters by how vulnerable they are to something (homers, steals, strikeouts). Click any column header to re-sort, and click a pitcher's name (HR or SB sheets) to see the batters/runners most likely to hurt him. The game filter at the top of the page now narrows these sheets too, so you can isolate one matchup. On the HR sheet, the Worst vs LHB / Worst vs RHB buttons re-sort to the starters most hittable by lefties or righties. Not bets, not financial advice.

💣 HR / Power Allowed — most homer-prone starters today

Ranked by Savant expected SLG-against (xSLG) — a stable season power-allowed metric. Pair a high xSLG with a HR-friendly Park HR% for the juiciest spots. wOBA vL / wOBA vR = how much damage the starter allows to lefty / righty bats specifically (higher = more hittable to that side); the Worst vs LHB/RHB buttons above sort by these. HR/9 vs-hand* is the model's small-sample platoon split (noisy — reference only). "—" = no Savant season sample joined. Click a pitcher → batters most likely to homer vs him.
🫱 Sort by hand vulnerability: — wOBA vL/vR = the SP's on-base+power allowed to lefty / righty bats today (switch hitters counted on the side they'll bat). Higher = more hittable to that side.

🏃 SB Allowed — easiest starters to run on today

🏃 Time window: — Multi-season = durable multi-year SB/opp rate (Razzball, 50+ IP arms only). This season = current-year SB-allowed leaders, including just-up / low-IP arms Razzball has no row for. Same starters either way; the page game filter still applies to both.
Ranked by realized SB/opp (steals allowed per opportunity — the per-chance rate, Razzball multi-yr). IP per SB = innings between steals allowed (a frequency stat, affected by how many runners reach base — so it won't perfectly track SB/opp; ∞ = none allowed). Read: RUN ≥ .110 · lean ≥ .085 · neutral ≥ .055 · tough ≥ .030 · AVOID below. Sav hold = current-yr Stealing Runs (+ = good hold). MLB rank is among all 216 qualified SPs. Click a pitcher → runners most likely to steal vs him.

The current-season stolen-base-allowed leaders among today's starters, ranked by SB/9 — including the low-IP / just-up arms the multi-year Razzball board has no row for (that's the gap this fills). SB / CS = steals and caught-stealings allowed this year · PK = pickoffs (a different way he kills the run game) · Att = SB+CS · CS% = how often he throws runners out · SB/9 = steals allowed per 9 IP (the usage-normalized rate; this feeds the runner board's P-SB when Razzball has no sample). Read bands are heuristic SB/9 (this season's league median ≈ 0.61): RUN ≥ 1.20 · lean ≥ 0.90 · neutral ≥ 0.50 · tough ≥ 0.25 · AVOID below. Click a pitcher → runners most likely to steal vs him.
